"Out of my way, boy!" Iron-shod hooves churned the earth to paste, and the youth removed his hand from his sword as the Unicorn sentry leapt away, his steed carrying him to safety. "I will remember," the Lion whispered, staring at the coward's back.
"Hitomi!" Hoshi screamed, the earth rocking beneath him as the ground shook with fury. "No, my brother," she replied, her voice as wind. "Our battle will have to wait for another time..."
"He was about to deal the killing blow, but my cousins dragged him away, calling my name…"

If you set your mind on the sword your mind is taken up with the sword. If you set your mind on timing, your mind is taken up with timing. In either case, your mind lingers and your action falters. Such is how noble men are put into the earth. - Takuan

The Brotherhood of the Tessen has always protected Toshi Ranbo, resenting the Lion and Crane's feuds. Now that the Lion and Crane battle once more, what will the Tessen do?"
"I have heard much of your prowess with the blade. It will be an honor defeating you."
The ninja’s speed countered the monk’s power. The two circled one another, looking for any weakness. Both knew only one would survive the encounter.

"Fortify that shrine," Tadenori commanded. "This will be our headquarters."
"Retreat!" Aoiko shouted, heaving Yobi over one shoulder.

Open: Choose your performing Courtier and target another player's Personality: Give him a Force penalty equal to your Courtier's Personal Honor. After each time he is assigned to attack this turn, gain 2 Honor.
designernotes: Courtier option to fight against both dishonor and military.  Idea behind story of card; the courtier charms his enemies, blunting their wrath or coming out from a social attack squeaky clean

text:
Reaction: After another player's action targets your Courtier: Negate his dishonoring from the action's effects.
Open: Choose your performing Courtier and target another player's Personality: Give him a Force penalty equal to your Courtier's Personal Honor. After each time he is assigned to attack this turn, gain 2 Honor.
title: Social Grace

flavor: The voices in his head convinced him of the certainty of his actions.
text: Open: Choose your performing Spirit Personality and target a Personality controlled by the active player: His controller must declare a normal attack this turn and assign the target (if legal).
designernotes: [Art Note: An animal spirit fights his enemy one on one.]

text: Open: Choose your performing Spirit Personality and target a Personality controlled by the active player: His controller must declare a normal attack this turn and assign the target (if legal).
title: Spiritual Manipulation

flavor: Samurai do not rout easily. Even in grim circumstances, they seize upon opportunities to prevail.
text: Reaction: Before a battle resolves, choose your performing opposed Samurai Personality at the current battlefield: Give him +2F.
designernotes: Surprise maneuver to swing the battle.

text: Reaction: Before a battle resolves, choose your performing opposed Samurai Personality at the current battlefield: Give him +2F.
title: Steady Resolve

flavor: To those on the Wall, nothing had changed. They fought and won, or fought and died, just as before.
text:
Reaction: After a Ranged or Melee Attack is targeted: Give it -3 strength.
Battle: Choose your performing defending Personality: Give him +5F. Straighten him before this battle resolves.
designernotes: Soldiers throwing large rocks at their enemies from atop ramparts.

text:
Reaction: After a Ranged or Melee Attack is targeted: Give it -3 strength.
Battle: Choose your performing defending Personality: Give him +5F. Straighten him before this battle resolves.
title: Strong Defenses

flavor: It is shameful to simply cause another to lose face. Courtiers hone their skills to cause others to dishonor themselves.
text: Limited: Bow your performing Courtier and target a Personality: Dishonor him. Draw a card.
designernotes: A Courtier gloating over a dishonored opponent.

text: Limited: Bow your performing Courtier and target a Personality: Dishonor him. Draw a card.
title: Subtle Games

flavor: "I did not realize you were a grasshopper!"
use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
text:
As a Focus Effect: After this duel ends, give its winner +3F.
Battle: Target two Personalities controlled by the same player at different locations: Switch their locations. If they moved, straighten their units.
designernotes: [Art Note: Someone using odd tactics to confuse his opponent.]

use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
title: Sudden Movement
flavor: Only a poor commander fails to account for sudden changes in an enemy's position.
text:
As a Focus Effect, give the duel's winner +3F.
Battle: Target two Personalities controlled by the same player at different locations. Switch their locations. Straighten both their units as they move.
designernotes: [Art Note: Someone using odd tactics to confuse his opponent.]

text:
As a Focus Effect, give the duel's winner +3F.
Battle: Target two Personalities controlled by the same player at different locations. Switch their locations. Straighten both their units as they move.
title: Sudden Movement

flavor: They were not prepared for this new mode of assault. The unprepared usually do not get another chance to prepare.
text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Tactician and target an enemy card without attachments: Destroy it.
designernotes: [Art Note: An unsuspecting Personality has fallen into a Lion trap, finding himself isolated. We can see his killer(s), unbeknownst to him, about ready to strike.]

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Tactician and target an enemy card without attachments: Destroy it.
title: Tactical Assault

flavor: "I trust there will be no questions," the Khan said dryly. There were no questions.
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
text: Battle/Open: Choose your performing Commander and target one to three Personalities controlled by the same player: Straighten zero or more of their attachments, then transfer zero or more attachments among any of the targets.
designernotes: The Governor of the Ivory Kingdoms reading a decree.

text: Battle/Open: Choose your performing Commander and target one to three Personalities controlled by the same player: Straighten zero or more of their attachments, then transfer zero or more attachments among any of the targets.
title: The Game Has Changed

flavor: "Earth abides. Earth endures. To understand earth, one must do the same." - Agasha Kurou
text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Monk and target another player's Personality: Move him to the current battlefield. If your Monk is not Earth, straighten the target's unit.
designernotes: Kiho to aid Ring of Earth

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Monk and target another player's Personality: Move him to the current battlefield. If your Monk is not Earth, straighten the target's unit.
title: The Lesson of Earth

flavor: Faith seems indestructible until even the slightest doubt enters the mind.
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
text:
Limited: Target a Celestial: Discard it.
Battle/Open: Target a Personality with 3 or higher Chi: Give him -2C.
designernotes: Uncertainty redone.  Sin cards: Something for Obsidian theme to play with later?

text:
Limited: Target a Celestial: Discard it.
Battle/Open: Target a Personality with 3 or higher Chi: Give him -2C.
title: Theological Indecision

flavor: Ronin will resort to baser methods should they lose their patience.
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
text: Limited: Bow your performing Personality or Follower and target a Personality without attachments and with lower Force, or target a Follower: Bow it.
designernotes: A man getting assaulted by masked strangers in an alley by an inn.

text: Limited: Bow your performing Personality or Follower and target a Personality without attachments and with lower Force, or target a Follower: Bow it.
title: Thuggish Techniques

flavor: "A samurai who embraces bushido will never be weak. When his body fails him, his spirit will rise up and strike." - Kitsu Sorano
text: Battle: Target your Paragon: Create a Personality with the same base Chi, Personal Honor and keywords as the target, and whose base Force equals that target's Personal Honor. Remove the Personality from the game after the battle ends.
designernotes: [Art Note: An honorable Samurai striking down a wicked, dishonorable enemy.]

text: Battle: Target your Paragon: Create a Personality with the same base Chi, Personal Honor and keywords as the target, and whose base Force equals that target's Personal Honor. Remove the Personality from the game after the battle ends.
title: Two-Fold Virtue

flavor: If the first strike is the last strike, then the victor is not always the strongest.
use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Personality and target an enemy card without attachments: Bow it. You may bow your Personality; if you did, draw a card.
designernotes: A Goblin jumping at a samurai from behind a rock.

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Personality and target an enemy card without attachments: Bow it. You may bow your Personality; if you did, draw a card.
title: Unexpected

flavor: "When steel comes out, you think of the fight. None of your sensei's pretty words will save you if you are not ready to kill." - Yamazaki
text: Battle/Open: Target a Personality: His controller may choose to bow him. If he did not choose this, he must pass at his next opportunity this phase to take a Battle, Limited, or Open action.
designernotes: [Art Note: A Samurai hesitates in battle, a hesitation which spells his doom.]

text: Battle/Open: Target a Personality: His controller may choose to bow him. If he did not choose this, he must pass at his next opportunity this phase to take a Battle, Limited, or Open action.
title: Unfortunate Hesitation

flavor: "Swordsmen are taught to watch their opponent's eyes. Around here, they should be watching the ground." - Hiruma Hikazu
text: Battle: If there is no Terrain in play at the current battlefield, bow your performing Scout and target an enemy card without attachments: Destroy it. Put this card into play there.
designernotes: A Scout luring an enemy unit into a treacherous mountain pass.

text: Battle: If there is no Terrain in play at the current battlefield, bow your performing Scout and target an enemy card without attachments: Destroy it. Put this card into play there.
title: Unstable Ground

flavor: The world has five smiles on one face.
text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Tattooed Personality, target a Tattoo Strategy in your discard pile, discard a card unless your Personality is Void, and remove this card from the game: Put the Tattoo into your hand. You may take an additional Battle action.
designernotes: A Dragon Monk using his Void Tattoo to focus his energy.

text: Void Battle: Target your performing unbowed Tattooed Personality, discard a card unless your Personality is Void, and remove this Strategy from the game to put a target a Tattoo Strategy in your discard pile into your hand. You may take an additional Battle action.
title: Void Tattoo

flavor: "Good commanders can anticipate the enemy. Great commanders already know what the enemy is going to do." - Akodo Furu
text: Reaction: After engaging, choose your performing Scout at the current battlefield and target a card in a unit there: You have Reconnaissance there. Abilities on cards (now) in the target's unit may not be used.
designernotes: Scout observes the enemy camp at night; the enemy leader must try to disguise his strategy by choosing carefully what to reveal and what to keep hidden.

text: Reaction: After engaging, choose your performing Scout at the current battlefield and target a card in a unit there: You have Reconnaissance there. Abilities on cards (now) in the target's unit may not be used.
title: Watchers in the Dark

flavor: "Not all dangers need be supernatural or military in nature, it seems. How dreadfully pedestrian." - Otomo Suikihime
text: Battle: Destroy any player's Terrain or Region at the current battlefield and target a Personality: His path is blocked. Bow him or move him home.
designernotes: [Art Note: Some aspect of the physical terrain is preventing a unit from getting to battle.]

text: Battle: Destroy any player's Terrain or Region at the current battlefield and target a Personality: His path is blocked. Bow him or move him home.
notes: Legal January 1st, 2013
Released to the 2012 Quarter 4 Organized Play winners
title: Sinkhole

flavor: "Seal the breach at all costs! We will seal it with our bodies if we must!"
text: Reaction: After an action resolves that moved a Personality to or from the current battlefield, choose your performing Personality: Move him to the current battlefield, or from the current battlefield to your home. Straighten his unit if he moved.
designernotes: Legal December 1, 2012

text: Reaction: After an action resolves that moved a Personality to or from the current battlefield, target your performing Personality. Move him to the current battlefield, or from the current battlefield to your home. Straighten his unit if this moved him.
notes: Legal December 1st, 2012
Released in the 2012 Summer Con pack
title: Closing the Gap

flavor: There is nothing within the Empire or the Colonies that cannot be granted by the will of the Divine Empress.
text: Limited: If it is not your first turn: Starting with you, each player may create an unbowed Holding with the trait "Bow this card: Produce 2 Gold."
designernotes: Legal December 1, 2012

text: Limited: If it is not your first turn: Starting with you, each player may create an unbowed Holding with the trait "Bow this card: Produce 2 Gold."
notes: Legal December 1st, 2012
Released in the 2012 Summer Con pack
title: Imperial Charter

flavor: When two souls meet on the field of battle, when honor's demands must be answered, when armies stand with bated breath… that is the birth of legends.
text: Battle/Open: Target a Unique Personality: Bow him.
designernotes: Legal December 1, 2012

text: Battle/Open: Target a Unique Personality: Bow him.
notes: Legal December 1st, 2012
Released in the 2012 Summer Con pack

flavor: "Crush the humans!"
text:
Limited: Target a Castle Holding: Permanently remove one of its non-Gold-producing traits or abilities.
Limited: If you control a Personality, pay 4 Gold: Create a 4F/2C/0PH Shadowlands • Nonhuman • Oni Personality. Lose 3 Honor.

Limited: Target a Castle Holding: Permanently remove one of its non-Gold-producing traits or abilities.

title: Through the Breach

flavor: Striking an enemy's holdings renders him unable to make war. Burn enough, and all that must be done is wait long enough for the inevitable to take place.
text: Open: Target a Fortress tile: Flip it face-up or face-down (unless it is a player's last face-up Fortress).

title: Wildfire

flavor: "And throughout its history, the Empire has seen the birth of villains, even those with godlike power." - The Tale of Rokugan III
designernotes: This is the 10,000th card

flavor: "With the death of two gods, a new era is born. The future is full of promise and uncertainty." - The Tale of Rokugan VIII
"With the death of two gods, a new era is born. The future is full of promise and uncertainty." - The Tale of Rokugan VIII

flavor: "With the death of two gods, a new era is born. The future is full of promise and uncertainty." - The Tale of Rokugan VIII

text:
Limited: If another player has 40 or more Honor, has five Rings in play, or you have -20 or lower Honor: The Dynasty Phase comes before the Attack Phase this turn.
Battle: Draw a card.
title: The Deciding Moment

flavor: "My lord, I know where they are heading."
designernotes: [Art Note: This is a Scout who is gathering all of the intel his army has learned about the enemy's defenses and making the best possible plan.]

text:
You may only play one copy of Advanced Intelligence per battle.
Reaction: After a Recon Strategy's action resolves: Draw one card for each action from a Recon Strategy you have taken this battle.
title: Advanced Intelligence

flavor: "Have you forgotten me, dog?"
designernotes: A duel for Crane/Dragon to snipe key defenders in the honor / dishonor matchup.  Is this too specialized? (only offers 4FV for military matchup)

text: Iaijutsu Revenge Reaction: After another player's performed Limited or Open action resolves which caused you an Honor loss, target your performing unbowed Duelist to target and have him challenge the action's performer. Destroy the loser. Give the winner two +1F Revenge tokens.
title: An Insult Answered

flavor: "This artifact is one of a kind, my friends. If you find my price unacceptable, perhaps we can make an arrangment for certain… favors?"

text: Battle: Target an enemy Personality whose unit's total Gold cost is 10 or lower: He defects. Take control of him (he joins your army). Dishonor him. Destroy any of his Followers whose Honor Requirement he now fails to meet. Give control of him to his owner before the turn ends.
title: Black Marketeering

flavor: The proliferation of Fudoism throughout the Colonies led to heated discussions among certain sects as to what the Brotherhood's response should be.
designernotes: [Art Note: A schism in the Brotherhood develops, causing problems wit their search for Enlightenment.]

text:
Open: Until the turn ends, Rings will not enter play if their owner has already put a Ring into play this turn.
Limited: Target an Event in another player's discard pile. End all of its ongoing effects. Shuffle it into its owner's deck.
title: Brotherhood Schism

flavor: At its zenith, the Obsidian Moon's faint light filtered in and highlighted the rapt, alert face of the dutiful yojimbo.
designernotes: A Yojimbo watching over their charge.

text:
As a Focus Effect, after this duel ends, straighten a Personality.
Battle/Open: Target your performing Yojimbo. The next time (this turn) another player's action would target one of your Personalities, it targets the Yojimbo instead, if legal. You may take an additional action.
title: Ceaseless Vigil

flavor: "Ships can be very dangerous places, and the sea gives no testimony. Other clans should talk softly in our waters, because they do not know the risks they run." - Yoritomo Iwashi

text: Open: Target another player's face-up Personality in or out of play: Permanently give him Overconfident (each other player may draw a card after an Overconfident card enters or leaves play).
title: Courting Trouble

flavor: "In times of peace, when coffers are filling and defense is on the minds of all just lords, why should the Kaiu not be called upon often? And why should we not receive just compensation for our time and skill?" - Kaiu Onizuka
designernotes: A straight resource trade, 1:1

text: Limited: Permanently give each of your provinces +1 strength.
title: Debt to the Kaiu

flavor: "Cut the bridge. Send scouts to find if there is another way reinforcements could reach the enemy."
designernotes: [Art Note: Something happens that prevents an attacking unit from being able to fight effectively.]

text: Battle: Target an attacking Follower or Personality without attachments: It does not contribute Force to its army's total Force this battle.
title: Defensive Blockade

flavor: "There is no room for doubt when you are guarding one of the Walls. Find your foe and destroy it. That is all there is." - Hida Watari
designernotes: An intense, close-quarters fight.

use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
title: Determined Challenge
flavor: "When a man faces an enemy stronger than himself, then you see the strength of his will." - Hida Kisada
text: Battle: Target your unbowed attacking Personality. Move a target enemy Personality at any location to the current battlefield. Straighten him as he moves.
designernotes: An intense, close-quarters fight.

text: Battle: Target your unbowed attacking Personality. Move a target enemy Personality at any location to the current battlefield. Straighten him as he moves.
title: Determined Challenge

flavor: "Our name is a common term. Some tend to forget the power it bespeaks." - Togashi Noboru
designernotes: [Art Note: This is akin to the old Dragon Tattoo that allowed you to breath fire.]

text: Fire Limited: Target your performing unbowed Tattooed Personality who has not performed an action from a Dragon Tattoo this game. He is imbued with the mystical power of the Dragon. Permanently give him the ability, "Fire Limited: Give this Personality a +1F Fire token."
title: Dragon Tattoo

flavor: Ratlings or Unicorn war-dogs would have been preferable to this disheveled man, but he would have to make do with him.
designernotes: A hung over Ronin scratching himself standing before a disgusted Samurai.

text: Battle, :g2:: Target your performing Personality. Create a 2F Ronin Follower and attach it to him.
title: Dubious Resources

flavor: "It matters not what it does, as long as it gives opportunity. Only in battle will my name shine." - Daigotsu Aya
designernotes: A Paragon getting ready for battle.

text:
As a Focus Effect, after this duel ends, bow the winner.
Limited: Target your performing Paragon. He may assign even if bowed. Give him Elite (Elite cards contribute Force even if bowed).
title: Embracing Battle

flavor: Ties of friendship, ties of duty, even ties of blood… all require effort to maintain over such distances as between the Empire and the Colonies.
designernotes: Two people writing letters to each other.

text: Open: Choose your performing unbowed Personality and target another player's unbowed Personality: Neither may perform actions this phase.
title: Exchange of Letters

flavor: Most clans value misdirection in war, at least in varying degrees. The Crab are unique in that they show their opponents exactly what to expect from them.
designernotes: Heads on pikes.

text:
As a Focus Effect: After this duel ends, if your Personality won it, you may give a Personality -4F.
Open: Target another player's Personality: After the next time this turn he is assigned to attack or is destroyed, gain 2 Honor.
title: Fair Warning

flavor: "Getting closer was your mistake."
designernotes: A Monk striking an attacker after drawing him in, Bruce Lee style

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Monk: After the next time (this turn) an action targets him, you may choose either to negate its effects on your Monk, or to bow the unit of one Personality that performed it after the action resolves. You may take an additional Battle action.
title: Grappling the Snake

flavor: "That is exactly what the reserves are for," he said coldly.
designernotes: A Commander rallying his exhausted troops for one last push.

text: Battle: Choose your performing Commander and target a Follower in your hand or discard pile: Attach it to him, ignoring costs. Destroy it before this battle resolves. You may take an additional Battle action.
title: Harsh Taskmaster

flavor: The Empress's presence calms the inflamed soul.
designernotes: [Art Note: A Scorpion attempts to slander a Crane in court but the Crane uses one of his many favors to shrug the attempt off.]

text:
Open: Target a Personality: After the next time this turn he is assigned to attack, dishonor him and his controller loses Honor equal to his base Personal Honor.
Open: Each of your provinces has +2 strength for each dishonorable Personality attacking it.
title: Imperial Protection

flavor: "Stay your blade, Yusugi. You will only justify him to the guards."
designernotes: A Scorpion Courtier pointing out someone to an Imperial guard.

text: Battle: Bow your performing Courtier at any location and target an enemy Personality: Bow him. Give this battlefield's province a strength bonus equal to your Courtier's Chi.
title: Incriminating Testimony

flavor: "A little humiliation will be good for them." - Bayushi Makubesu
designernotes: A Magistrate stopping a Samurai as he exits a building.

text: Limited: Choose your performing unbowed Magistrate and target one or two Personalities: Dishonor them. Draw an additional card during your next End Phase.
title: Ingenuity

flavor: "Nice trick." - Yoritomo Akuhiko
designernotes: [Art Note: A Samurai, having seen a trick his enemy just used against his comrade, now uses the same trick against that enemy.]

text: Battle: Choose your performing Personality and a Battle Strategy in another player's discard pile: Its abilities may be used an additional time this turn. Your Personality copies a keyword on an enemy Personality. You may take an additional action to play the Strategy for one of its actions. After it resolves, remove it from the game.
title: Learning

flavor: "We are all capable of wonders, but so few of us are capable of believing it. It requirse discipline and practice to become what we are." - Asako Karachu
designernotes: An Asako Henshin running as fast as a horse.

text: Open: Choose your performing unbowed Henshin and target a Personality: Permanently give him, or remove from him, Cavalry, Naval, or Tactician.
title: Manipulate Energies

flavor: Whether one warrior to another, or one army to another, the battlefield can be used for treachery as well as violence.
designernotes: A Samurai keeps an enemy distracted, making him miss the fight.

text: Limited: Choose your performing Samurai and target another player's Personality: Neither Personality may be assigned to battlefields.
title: Martial Manipulation

flavor: It is often difficult for a ninja's opponent to discern which attacks are merely distractions.
designernotes: [Art Note: Should show a Ninja attacking and then slipping into the shadows, once again making him undetectable.]

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Ninja: Ranged 4 Attack. The next time this battle the enemy leader takes a Battle action, if it can legally target the Ninja, it must target him.
title: Metsubushi

flavor: "Your deeds have gained much glory for the clan, my friend. People, important people, have taken notice."
designernotes: A Tactician responding to a change in the battle.

text: Open: Choose your performing Tactician and target your Personality: He may assign even if bowed. Give him Elite (Elite cards contribute Force even if bowed).
title: Military Promotion

flavor: "Make sure he is given all that he requires in order to discharge his duty."
designernotes: A Samurai talking to a Courtier.

use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
title: My Lord's Favor
flavor: "For one's worth to be recognized by one's own lord is one of the most sublime pleasures a samurai can enjoy." - Akodo's Leadership
text:
Political Open: Target your Personality. Take the Imperial Favor.
Favor Political Battle/Open: Discard the Imperial Favor to straighten your target Personality's unit.
designernotes: A Samurai talking to a Courtier.

text:
Political Open: Target your Personality. Take the Imperial Favor.
Favor Political Battle/Open: Discard the Imperial Favor to straighten your target Personality's unit.
title: My Lord's Favor

flavor: "No one sees what is not there." - Goju Yurishi
designernotes: Goju Yurishi on a stick.

text: Limited: Choose your performing unbowed Personality with the base Ninja keyword: He infiltrates the enemy ranks. Permanently give him the trait, "After a Straighten Phase begins: Choose your bowed non-Ninja Personality. Negate his straightening this phase." Give control of him to another player.
title: Only a Shadow

flavor: "Color your words any way you like, Lion, but the Unicorn will not cede control of the Ki-Rin's Path to you or anyone else! The Empress has given us our task, and we shall fulfill it ourselves!"
designernotes: Some jungle animal fleeing in front of a Spider Conqueror in the Colonies.

text: Open: Target another player's Personality: Permanently give him Brash (the Defender may draw a card after a Brash card is assigned to attack).
title: Out of My Sight!

flavor: "There will come a time when you will find yourself at the mercy of enemies, and to survive and fulfill your duty, you will need aid. Do not be so proud that the rest of us must carry your burden after your death." - Tsuruchi Nobukatsu
designernotes: A Scout gesturing to a larger group to come help him.

text: Battle: Bow your performing Scout and target a face-up Scout Personality in your province: Bring the target into play at the current battlefield, paying all costs. If you have Reconnaissance, you may take an additional Battle action.
title: Requesting Reinforcements

flavor: Hida Kaiji leapt from the tower, plummeting nearly one hundred feet to attack the things that had emerged from the jungle. He landed unharmed. His battle cry, his comrades knew, was as much anguish over his survival as rage.
designernotes: [Art Note: Reinforcements arriving to battle, though it should show a Berserker or Oni pushing his way through, as they don't even demand payment, they just want to go kill things.]

text: Battle: Choose your performing Personality: The next time this battle another player's Battle action would target one of your cards, it targets your performer, if legal. If he has 7 or more base Force, you may take an additional Battle action (after this action ends).
title: Ruthless Onslaught

flavor: "When one prepares for war, one should be prepared to see anything. Anything." - Kitsu Sorano
designernotes: One special spirit standing out from the others.

text: Open: If it is not your turn and the active player controls any Personalities, choose your performing unbowed Spirit: After the next Dynasty Phase begins, if it was opposed this turn, gain 2 Honor, and if no units were assigned to attack this turn, gain 3 Honor if your Honor is lower than each other player's.
title: Sentinel Spirit

flavor: "Honorable samurai never gossip, of course. They simply share stories about interesting people." - Doji Dainagon
designernotes: Hilarity ensues when both players have these.

text: Limited: Choose your performing unbowed Courtier: After this action ends (and this card is in your discard pile), gain 1 Honor for each card titled Sharing Gossip in all players' discard piles.
title: Sharing Gossip

flavor: "The differences from traditional formations are subtle, but important. The strength of this form is magnified tenfold, and that is why we use it." - Akodo Uehara
designernotes: [Art Note: A line of Spearmen set themselves up in a defensive charge, waiting for the charge.]

text: Battle: Target your defending Personality: Give him and each of his Followers +3F.
title: Shield Formation

flavor: "If you are unsteady, so too is the ground you touch."
designernotes: A group of scouts hiding from their enemies behind a large boulder and/or brushes.

text:
Battle: If there is no Terrain in play at the current battlefield: Put this card into play there.
Reaction: If this card is in play, when another player's action would target one of your Personalities, choose your performing Personality: The action targets him instead, if legal.
title: Shifting Terrain

flavor: "Better to get revenge another day," she said philosophically.
designernotes: A Samurai retreating.

text:
Limited: Put this card into play. Discard all your other Kata in play.
Battle: If this card is in play, choose your performing Personality: Move him home. If he moved, straighten his unit.
title: Sound the Retreat

flavor: "We cannot wield a katana, but we do not need one to deal with you, wretch!"
designernotes: A Rokugani child throwing rocks at a Samurai with a slingshot.

text: Battle: Ranged 1 Attack. If the Ranged Attack destroyed a card, draw a card; if it destroyed a Follower, you may take an additional Battle action.
title: Sticks and Stones

flavor: "I witnessed it myself," the samurai-ko said calmly, despite the murmurs.
designernotes: Anyone of sufficient status can offer testimony

text: Limited: Choose your performing unbowed honorable Personality and target a Personality with lower base Personal Honor: Dishonor or rehonor the target.
title: Testimony

flavor: He had just enough time to see the blur before his world narrowed to a single, red-black point.
designernotes: Draft slot, should be a common

text: Battle: Ranged 3 Attack with +1 strength for each Follower in the target's unit.
title: The Arrow's Eye

flavor: "What did you seek here?"
designernotes: [Son of Satsu] turning away from a pilgrim.

text:
As a Focus Effect: You may discard your Personality in this duel (ending the duel without resolution).
Battle: Even if you control no units at the current battlefield, choose your performing Monk at any location and bow your target Ring: Give the current battlefield's province +3 or -3 strength.
title: Turning Away

flavor: "Yes, the story of your cousin's battle is quite entertaining," Bayushi Shibata said. "Equally entertaining is the story of what he was doing in that district at the time. Allow me to tell it."
designernotes: [Art Note: A courtier heralds the exploits of an honorable warrior of his clan, only to have a Scorpion courtier slander the warrior, undoing any benefits.]

text: Reaction: Before a Proclaim action resolves: Negate its effects.
title: Turning the Story

flavor: He was the first to draw his sword, but he was not the last.
designernotes: Now you can put weapons with useful abilitieis on Kensai with useful abilities

text: Battle: Choose your performing Kensai: You may take two additional Battle actions from cards in his unit. Other actions will not give you new additional Battle actions this battle.
title: Two Hands

flavor: A Wasp never strikes a single time.
designernotes: A Tsuruchi archer shooting two arrows in close succession.

text:
Battle: Bow your performing Personality or Follower: Ranged 4 Attack.
Battle: If this card is in your discard pile, bow your performing Personality or Follower: Ranged 4 Attack. Remove this card from the game.
title: Two Stings

flavor: "Spend little time on the plan, for the only thing that is certain is that everything will go wrong." - Tangen's Lies
designernotes: A shoddy wall.

text: Battle: Target an enemy Personality or Follower: Give it -4F.
title: Unexpected Obstacle

flavor: Hesitation, particularly when justified, can be disastrous.
designernotes: Monks arguing about Fudoism.

text:
Open: Target a Ring which did not last enter play by its own text: This is not true enlightenment. Permanently remove its abilities.
Limited: Target one or more cards in your provinces: Discard them. Refill the provinces face-up from the bottom of your deck.
title: Unsettling Doubts

flavor: The clamor rose as everyone strove to be heard first.
designernotes: [Art Note: A Courtier is desperately trying to stall while a Courtier from a military clan is doing everything he can to end this court session so he can attack.]

text:
Open: Each player may only take two more non-Reaction actions this phase.
Limited: Pay 2 Gold: Draw a card.
title: Urgency

flavor: "Now it is the Mantis who have no time for your foolishness, old man. Did you think we had forgotten your insult?"
designernotes: Chi often has little to do in the draft environment; thought I'd give those lowF/highC guys something to do in a fight.

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Personality and target an enemy Personality with lower Chi: Move him home.
title: Vengeful Dismissal

flavor: Charity and virtue are often one.
designernotes: [Art Note: An Utaku Battle Maiden and a Matsu paragon come across some starving peasants who are in desperate need of koku to survive. Gee, I wonder which one will donate more money. ;)]

text: Open: If it is not your turn, choose another player: You come across some starving peasants in need. You, then the player, each pay zero or more Gold. Whoever paid more Gold gains Honor equal to the difference between the two amounts paid, or 4, whichever is less.
title: Virtuous Charity

flavor: "The valley has been the sight of a hundred battles over a thousand years, but somehow it always endures. It is beyond destruction." - Hida Kisada
designernotes: Grabbed from August Random ideas

text:
Before an action's effect destroys a card in a unit: Negate the destruction.
Battle: If there is no Terrain in play at the current battlefield: Put this card into play there.
title: Wide Valley

flavor: "We know you were involved in the blighting of our lands, Spider. That you will not confess is meaningless. Now we have a much more important question, and one you will answer: what weapon brought about this renewal?"
designernotes: [Note: This is the Lion investigating that the Spider have done something to their lands]

text:
Reaction: Before another player's action resolves: Negate its effects that bow, destroy, or discard your Holdings, Regions, or Terrains in play.
Open: Pay 2 Gold: Draw a card.
notes: Legal July 1st, 2013
Released in the 2013 Kotei pack
title: Interrogation

flavor: "Assemble your finest assassins, officers. I want to see this upstart general dead on the ground. I want to see his men weeping and tearing at their clothes. I want them broken." - Yogo Haruto
"Ready yourselves," Kusumi commanded. "This is the most important assignment you will ever receive. Those of you who survive will find yourselves well and truly elevated."

Jikaro's blade danced through the air, mere inches above Utoro's head. The Scorpion warrior rolled across the ground, lashing out at the Phoenix's legs, but his blade fell short. Each knew that they would face one another again.

text: Limited: Target your performing Personality. He undergoes vigorous training. Permanently give him Elite. (Elite cards contribute Force even if bowed.)
title: Elite Training

flavor: "We do not know why the Lion have attacked the Spider encampments with our advisors. They have brought no testimony, levied no charges. While we find the truth of the matter, we cannot simply allow this to go unanswered." - Mirumoto Konjirue
designernotes: Kensai (Good for all Kensai)

text: Battle/Open: Target your Personality at any location. He centers himself. Negate other players' cards' effects that prevent him assigning, moving, or performing actions. If he is a Kensai, straighten his unit.
title: A Cleansing Breath

flavor: He charged into the rain of arrows without hesitation. His trust in his armorer was absolute.
designernotes: [Note: An arrow harmlessly bounces of some armor.]

text:
Reaction: After a Personality with an Armor is targeted by a Melee or Ranged Attack, negate its effects.
Battle: Give a target Personality with an attachment +2F.
title: A Close Call

flavor: "You were warned, and yet you persisted."
designernotes: Political negotiations deteriorating into violence.

text: Favor Political Limited: Target your performing unbowed non-Duelist Courtier Personality and discard the Imperial Favor to target a Personality with equal or lower Personal Honor. Destroy a non-Unique card in his unit without attachments. Dishonor him (if he is still in play).
title: A Threat Enacted

flavor: With the government of the Colonies distracted by the events at the Second City, skirmishes among the Great Clans became increasingly frequent, and increasingly deadly.
designernotes: A Scout waiting in ambush for his target to pass.

text: Battle: Bow your target performing Personality to make a Ranged 3 Attack with +2 strength if your performer is a Scout. You may move him home.
title: Ambush Tactics

flavor: An oath to avenge fallen comrades is gravely serious. Nothing else can exist in the warrior's mind once the promise is spoken. A samurai's word and deed are as one.
use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
designernotes: A bloody battlefield.

text: Battle: Straighten your target performing Personality. If another player's Battle action has destroyed one of your Personalities this battle, negate the performer's next destruction from cards' effects (this turn).
title: Avenging the Fallen

flavor: "Bamboo endures, and so do I."
designernotes: [Note: This is Bamboo Tattoo, from the RPG.]

text: Limited: Target your performing unbowed Tattooed Personality to permanently give him Stalwart (Stalwart cards negate their first bowing each turn by other players' cards).
title: Bamboo Tattoo

flavor: "No, Bayushi-san, I could not send you out into that terrible rain. I will pour you a cup of tea, and we can discuss the Tao." - Kitsuki Yataku
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
designernotes: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)

text: Limited: Target a bowed Personality: Put this card into play. While this card remains in play, negate the target's straightening, and his controller has the ability, "Open: Pay 4 Gold: Discard a Bonds of Hospitality."
title: Bonds of Hospitality

flavor: "No member of the Empire may be tainted without their consent. But now that war has come, how many are secretly begging my father for his blessings?" - Daigotsu Kanpeki
designernotes: Designer's Choice

text: Limited: Bow your target performing Personality to permanently give him Shadowlands and three +1F tokens, straighten him before the turn ends, and lose 2 Honor.
title: Calling the Darkness

flavor: The berserkers of the Crab leap into the heart of danger, caution thrown to the wind. This tactic rarely works for those without their insanity.
use: 7 base force (Good for Berserkers and Onis)
designernotes: [Note: Generic Berserker battle action, rushing to a battle he is late to]

text: Absent Battle: Target your performing Personality at any location who either is at, assigned to, or would be opposed at, the current battlefield. Move him there. If his printed Force is 7 or higher, straighten him.
title: Charge Into Danger

flavor: "A proper guest always offers a gift, to show appreciation to his host." - Lady Doji
designernotes: [Note: A Samurai performing a Courteous act.]

text: Limited: Bow your target performing Samurai, target a face-up card in a Province, and choose another player. He may draw a card. Reduce the second target's Gold Cost (this turn) by 2, or 4 if the player drew a card.
title: Courteous Gift

flavor: The arrival of the Imperial forces touched off a series of events both predictable and amazing. In all quarters of the Second City, inside and out, samurai prepared to defend honor and clan the best way they knew how. - Chronicle of the Orchid Lands, Part I
use: Battle Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
designernotes: A Samurai getting ready to defend himself against an enemy charge.

text: Battle: Straighten your target performing bowed Personality and give him +3F.
title: Defensive Stance

flavor: "Clearly, claims must be checked, but this is getting out of hand. No sooner do we resolve one claim dispute when two more take its place! It is as if some malevolent force is directing it all." - Kitsuki Jakuei
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements), should represent the drained resources the Dragon are having to deal with due to the increasing beauracracy in the Second City
designernotes: [Note: The Kitsuki are having to deal with drained resources dealing with all of the bureaucracy in the Colonies.]

text:
Open: Until this phase ends or a player has discarded two cards from this effect, after each action other than this one resolves, its player discards a card.
Open: Draw a card.
title: Drain of Effort

flavor: "I would have preferred to go to the next world without ever spilling the blood of your noble people. Unfortunately for us both, my duty demanded otherwise."
designernotes: A beaten and bloody Yojimbo standing their ground.

text: Battle, :gstar:: If he would be opposed, Recruit your target performing discarded Yojimbo, paying 3 less Gold if you control a Courtier or Shugenja.

flavor: "Victory in a duel is the purest kind." - Doji Genshin
designernotes: Duel

text: Battle: Choose your performing unbowed Duelist and target an enemy Personality: Your Personality duels the target. Give the winner two +1F tokens. Destroy the duel's loser.

flavor: "Very satisfying when they realize we were there all along."
designernotes: [Note: Some additional troops are hiding in the woods, waiting for the right time to reveal themselves.]

text: Reaction: After engaging, choose your performing Scout Personality at the current battlefield: You have Reconnaissance there. Attach a Follower to him from your hand or discard pile, paying all costs. If you did, draw an additional card during your next End Phase.

flavor: "The young learn too late that they ought to learn from the past." - Moto Taha
use: Action Phase (no keyword requirements, should be slightly weaker than those with requirements)
designernotes: An official in the Second City looking over notes.

text: Open: Target another player's Sensei Personality: Remove all traits and abilities from him (this turn).
title: Forgotten Teachings

flavor: One generation raised during a time of war, and another raised during a time of peace. The differences in philosophy can be significant, and even family bonds can be tested.
designernotes: [Note: Satsu and his son are disagreeing over the proper way to handle some difficulties in the Ise Zumi order]

text: Open: Target two Personalities with the same Family name: They may not be assigned to the same battlefield.

flavor: "We will return every fire to them tenfold." - Moshi Rukia
use: Attacking Terrain, the waters outside of Kalani's landing, where the Crane are attacking the Mantis
designernotes: The waters outside Kalani's Landing, where the Crane are attacking the Mantis.

text:
Negate Personalities' movement to or from this battlefield.

flavor: "It is a simple truth that so many seekers would ignore - we do not search for enlightenment. We are enlightened; we simply do not realize it." - Yung
designernotes: A monk glowing with energy.

text: Open: Choose your performing Monk: Permanently give him the trait, "You may bow this card instead of bowing a Ring to pay a cost."

flavor: "Go and bring law to the lawless." - Utaku Ji-Yun

designernotes: The Empress dispatching two Imperial Legions to deal with the Colonies.

text: Open: Choose your performing Personality: The Empress dispatches him to the Colonies. Negate other players' cards' effects that prevent him assigning. After the next time this turn an attack is declared, straighten his unit.

designernotes: [Note: Someone attempting to cross a Clan border gets stopped and turned around because he does not have the proper paperwork.]

text: Reaction: Before an action resolves, target a Personality: Negate his movement from the action's effects.

flavor: No samurai climbs quite so high that he can safely disregard propriety. Carelessness is the fortunate man's undoing.

designernotes: A Samurai committing an embarrassing but not lethal social faux pas.

text: Limited: Target another player with 30 or more Family Honor: He commits a public breach of etiquette. He loses 5 Honor.

text: Battle: Bow your performing Courtier at any location and target one or more attacking cards, up to half of your Courtier's Chi, rounded down: Bow the targets.

use: Magistrate (Good for Crane, Dragon, Phoenix, and Scorpion), the Dragon suspect that maybe the Lion are right and that the Spider are up to no good
designernotes: The Dragon suspect that maybe the Lion are right about the Spider.

use: Paragon (good for all Paragons, does not have to be stat based)
designernotes: A Paragon inspiring others in death.

text: Battle/Open: Destroy your performing Paragon and target one or two of your other Personalities: Give them +3F and Stalwart (Stalwart cards negate their first bowing each turn by other players' cards).

text: Battle: Give two or more target opposed Personalities, each with a different Clan Alignment, +2F (each).

designernotes: The Crab attacking the Spider.

text: Battle: Target two Personalities in the same army that do not share a Clan alignment: Give each target -3F.

text:
Open: If it is the active player's first or second turn: No Attack Phase may be declared.

text:
You may include up to seven copies of this card in your deck.

If it has no attachments and you have played two or more other Pierce the Tapestry this battle, destroy it. If this did not destroy it, you may take an additional Battle action to play a Pierce the Tapestry.

flavor: "We have little information on their exact strength, but we must assume it is a complete Imperial Legion and plan accordingly." - Akodo Chiyo
use: Tactician (Good for all Tacticians), should be about the Lion and Crab preparing for the potential invasion
designernotes: Lion and Crab Tacticians preparing for the potential invasion.

text: Open: Choose your performing Personality and target a province: While at the province's battlefields, other players' Personalities and Followers have -1F and, if your performer is a Tactician, your Personalities and Followers have +1F.

text: Open: Destroy your target non-Unique Holding: Create a 3F/3C/2PH Samurai Personality.

text: Open: If (and only if) this card is in your discard pile, remove it from the game and target a player: He must choose and bow one of his unbowed Personalities, if possible. Gain 1 Honor.

designernotes: An Asako Henshin focusing to redistribute his strength.

flavor: All things echo in ways man cannot perceive.
designernotes: Two spirits merging.

text: Battle/Open: Destroy your performing Spirit Personality and target another Spirit Personality: Give it a permanent Force bonus equal to the performer's Force. It permanently copies the performer's abilities which do not themselves copy abilities. You may take an additional action.

flavor: "Surely my lord will not notice if a few baubles go missing."
designernotes: A Samurai in the Colonies stumbling upon some valuable artifact.

text:
After a battle resolution ends in which you destroyed an army or province: Draw a card.

text: Battle: Destroy your performing unbowed Courtier and target an enemy Personality: Dishonor him. You may target and destroy a card without attachments in his unit.

flavor: "A samurai's mind may desire peace, but his heart aches for war." - Daigotsu Kanpeki
designernotes: The Crab attacking the Dragon in the Colonies.

text:
Before this battle resolves, you may move one of your opposed attacking Personalities at this battlefield to an adjacent, unresolved battlefield.

flavor: The request had come easily to his lips, and the answer now lay in his hand. The pouch weighed heavily, burdened by the expectation of the future.
designernotes: A shady-looking Samurai bribing an official in the Colonies.

flavor: "When the time comes to fight, cast yourself into battle with no thought for your own survival." - Akodo's Leadership
designernotes:
Art: A strong samurai in the midst of battle.
Design: a basic Force bonus.

title: A Warrior's Brutality
flavor: The demeanor that a warrior presents can be every bit as important as the skill with which he wields his blade. An enemy that is demoralized before the battle begins is an enemy that has already been defeated.
text: Battle: Give your target opposed Personality +3F.
designernotes:
Art: A strong samurai in the midst of battle.
Design: a basic Force bonus.

title: A Warrior's Brutality
flavor: No matter how noble, or graceful, or honorable, in the end a samurai is just a killer. He is a slayer of men. Every thought, every breath, every waking moment is devoted to the cause of killing and dying. The duty of a samurai is not only to attend to the needs of his lord… it is to make of himself a blade for the hands of his master.
text: Battle: Give your target opposed Personality +3F.

title: A Warrior's Brutality
flavor: The Code of Bushido dictates that courtesy and compassion are virtues all samurai should extol. Many warriors often forget these, however, in favor of the glory of battle.

flavor: A late arrival is sometimes forgiven if it turns the tide against the enemy.
designernotes: A Samurai riding through the night to get to wherever it is he's going on time.

text: Battle: Give Reserve to your target Personality in a Province. (You may Recruit a Reserve card, if it would be opposed, as an Absent Battle action.)

flavor: The rivalry between the Kakita and the Mirumoto will likely never be resolved, but each generation tries anew.
designernotes: Two samurai engaging in an exhibition duel.

text: Iaijutsu Political Limited: Your target unbowed Personality challenges another player's target Personality. Each Personality must focus exactly once, if possible. The winner draws a card. Dishonor the loser.

flavor: "Love is a game in Rokugan's courts… but sometimes the game costs both players their hearts, or even their lives." - Doji Shizue
designernotes:
Art: Profile of a Samurai takes up the left foreground, staring at a beautiful maiden in the garden who is surrounded by others, unapproachable and distant.
Design: Reimagining Unrequited Love.

text: Political Open: Bow your target unbowed Human Personality. Target another player's unbowed Human Personality. His controller may bow him and gain 1 Honor. If he did not choose this, dishonor both targets, and both their controllers lose 3 Honor.
title: Desire

flavor: "We cannot afford bad decisions. We cannot afford failure. The Yodotai must not become interested in the Colonies, and we must do everything we can to make sure they do not." - Akodo Kano
designernotes: Art Note: Someone's plan working out in battle.

title: Directing the Battle
flavor: "I soon found that I became a much better general the moment I abandoned all planning." - Akodo Kano
text: Battle, [4]: If there are any enemy units at this battlefield, search your Fate deck for a non-unique Battle Strategy. Show it and put it in your hand.
designernotes: Art Note: Someone's plan working out in battle.

text: Battle, :g4:: If there are any enemy units at this battlefield, search your Fate deck for a non-unique Battle Strategy. Show it and put it in your hand.
title: Directing the Battle

flavor: Kanshi was the samurai's final means of protest. Where all words would fail, the pinnacle of persuasion was a quiet death.
designernotes:
Art: I'd love to see this one refer to Denton's epic story Kanshi; a female Crab Samurai removing her outer robe to reveal she has made the three cuts, shaming her lord for compromsing the defense of the Wall.
Design: A way to commit seppuku to shame someone.

text: Political Open: Your target unbowed Human Personality commits seppuku. A target player loses Honor equal to the Personality's Personal Honor or 4, whichever is lower.
title: Dying Remonstration

flavor: A kyuden's gardens are often as memorable as the palace itself, weighing heavily in the minds of potential dignitaries. Rival lords are always expanding their grounds, waging polite wars to outdo one another.
designernotes: Art: Someone tending to the gardens.

text: Limited: Give one or two target Gold-producing Holdings +1 Gold Production each (this turn).
title: Expanding the Gardens

flavor: When need drives, few samurai care about expenses.
designernotes: A high ranking Samurai yelling at someone for the slow pace of rebuilding.

text:
Discipline :g2: (You may pay 2 Gold to play this Discipline from your discard pile, then remove it from the game after its action resolves.)
Battle: Give this Province +2 or -2 Strength.
title: Expensive Achievement

flavor: "Anyone can find their center in the quiet of a temple. Only one who truly understands the flow of the elements can do it in the tumult of battle." - Togashi Noburo
designernotes: Art Note: A Monk centering himself in the middle of battle.

text:
Limited: Put this Kata into play. Discard all your other Kata in play.
Battle: If this card is in play, straighten a Ring.
title: Find Your Center

flavor: "The madness of P'an Ku, the squabbles between the Commander and the Governor, the uproar over the Fudo cult… none of it matters when you have steel in your hands. Then there is only the present moment and the strike that defines it." - Mirumoto Hatsuto

title: Grasp the Swords
flavor: "Arm yourself however you can. If one carries oneself with honor, there is no shame in defending one's lord." - Mirumoto's Niten
text: Battle: Give your target Personality with a Weapon +2F/+2C. If he is a Kensai, you may take an additional action.
Battle: Give your target Personality with a Weapon +2F/+2C. If he is a Kensai, you may take an additional action.

flavor: The Colonies had seen much chaos over the years, and the bloodshed was not yet done.
designernotes:
Art: A battle in the jungle, in which troops charging through a bamboo thicket surprise a samurai already locked in combat.
Design: Stand Against the Waves as a terrain.

text:
Personalities without Followers have -2F. Commanders have +1F.

flavor: "The followers of Fudo may be slightly less mad than P'an Ku's victims, but they are no less dangerous. It is our task to root out all who harbor forbidden texts, and we will not be stopped. Incidentally, if you do not remember to bring all of your spells, you cannot stop us." - Asako Misora
designernotes: Art Note: A Shugenja shows up to battle to find one of his scrolls has been stolen.

text: Engage: Bow a target enemy attachment.
title: Lost Equipment

flavor: "My new orders would be far more likely to be obeyed if I could read them." - Hida Kurabi
designernotes: A set of orders ruined by water and mud.

flavor: Even the words of a sacred Emperor can be lost, if they are exposed to the elements improperly.

flavor: "An unfortunate accident, my friend."
designernotes: Merchants working overtime.

title: Mercantile Conflict
flavor: "Yes, I did hear about the fire at the inn across town. It's a tragedy, and I hope his family can survive the loss. In the meantime, you are fortunate, because we do have rooms available."
text:

flavor: "Soon, we may not be able to engage the Spider without political ramifications. We must act, and quickly, before the opportunity escapes us."

Design: was meta for [Night Raid] (above); now an anti-Cavalry card

flavor: "Crab-san, I am not interested in listening to you blather about your clan's 'slighted honor.' If you will not let me drink in peace, you will give me the satisfaction of a rousing brawl, and as long as I have a hand, I can find a weapon." - Mirumoto Hatsuto
designernotes: A bushi grabbing a bottle to defend himself with during a bar fight.

text: Battle, :g5:: Discard a card to create a Weapon Item with a Force Modifier equal to the discarded card's Focus Value and attach it to your target Personality.

flavor: "The Crab do not know enough to let sleeping horrors lie. I am going to enjoy this." - Goju Obayashi

text: Ninja Limited, :g10:: Destroy a target Personality with 3 or lower Chi, or with 4 or lower Chi if you control a Ninja Personality. Lose Honor equal to your Starting Family Honor.

flavor: "Be on your guard," he warned. "He is impatient, and I may prod him into a foolish reaction."
designernotes: Art: A Yojimbo training.

designernotes:
Art: an homage to Blood Money.
Design: A one-shot Blood Money, lets you splash out of Clan in draft.

designernotes: A Mantis Magistrate planting evidence.

designernotes: Art Note: A Samurai leaves the battle, drawing some troops out with him.

flavor: Cowardice is a common accusation, but even Akodo's Leadership teaches that discretion is often the path to victory.

flavor: "I probably should have thought that through…"

flavor: The soldiers of the Imperial Legion are among the finest in Rokugan. A single legion's support can turn the tide of a war.
designernotes: A unit of Imperial archers on the battlefield.

text:
Battle: Ranged 1 Attack.
Favor Political Battle: Discard the Imperial Favor to make a Ranged 3 Attack.
title: The Legion's Might

flavor: "Kuni Renyu was not totally satisfied with the peace brought by the defeat of P'an Ku and sought to make his dissatisfaction known to Togashi Noburo. Noburo would not yield the point, and so the Crab and Dragon were slowly but surely brought into conflict." - Asako Chukage

text: Absent Battle: If he would be opposed, move your target Personality at home to the current battlefield. If he is a Monk, give him +2F.
title: The Way of the World

flavor: "In true combat, self-preservation cannot be your goal. Every thought, every motion, indeed every breath, must be spent only towards the destruction of your enemy." - Akodo's Leadership
designernotes: Art Note: A Samurai sacrificing himself so his friends can escape.

text: Battle: Target your unbowed Personality. Target an enemy unit with Force equal to or lower than your Personality's Force, or Force plus Personal Honor if he is defending. Destroy both targets.
title: Thoughtless Sacrifice

flavor: "Calm your mind," he cautioned the young warrior. "The secret of the universe lies where the world stands still."
designernotes: A Monk meditating.

text:
Discipline :g2: (You may pay the Discipline cost to play this card from the discard pile. Remove it from the game after the action resolves.)
Limited, :g1:: Give your target face-up Personality out of play Destined. (Draw a card after your Destined card enters play.)
title: Two Steady Breaths

flavor: "You should fear the wrath of the Kuni," he said quietly. "Let me remind you why."
designernotes: Art: A Shugenja getting out another scroll to lay waste to an oncoming Samurai/army.

text:
Limited: Put this Kata into play. Discard all your other Kata in play with different titles.
Battle, :gstar:: If this card is in play, Equip a Spell to your target unbowed, opposed Shugenja. You may take an additional action.
title: Unchecked Fury

flavor: "A wild beast may be tamed," he snarled, "but that makes it no less dangerous. You would be wise to consider that before you speak to me again."
designernotes: A Spider Clan member cutting a practice dummy in half.

title: Unholy Strike
flavor: "This kata is magnificent. I eagerly await the opportunity to try it on a target more worthy of my ire."
text:
Battle: Melee 2 Attack.
Interrupt: Give a Melee or Ranged Attack +2 strength.
designernotes: A Spider Clan member cutting a practice dummy in half.

text:
Battle: Melee 2 Attack.
Interrupt: Give a Melee or Ranged Attack +2 strength.
title: Unholy Strike

flavor: "In my life, I have often found that honor is not a guarantee of virtue." - The Writings of Agasha Kitsuki
designernotes:
Art: A Dragon Magistrate angrily shouting and pointing his finger across the battlefield at a startled and embarrassed enemy Lion Clan samurai
Design: Magistrates are the most likely to get into battles against dishonorable foes.

text: Battle: Give a target Personality a Force penalty equal to his printed Personal Honor. If he is dishonorable, bow him.
title: Unpleasant Truths

flavor: "There are three aspects to making an unlikely alliance work. First, know his strengths. Second, know his weaknesses. Last, and most important, know when to stay out of his way." - Bayushi Makubesu
designernotes: A courtier mocking a mixed clan force.

text: Political Open: Bow your target unbowed Courtier. All Personalities have -1F for each Clan Alignment in their army (this turn).
title: Unseemly Alliance

flavor: "Nothing warms my heart more than a show of confidence from my fellow warriors."

text: Battle: If he would be opposed, move your target unbowed Personality at home to the current battlefield. If he is Cavalry, Scout, or a Clan Champion, you may take an additional action to use a printed ability on a card in his unit.
title: A Champion's Tactics

flavor: "A true samurai always grasps his sword - if not with his hand, then with his soul. Should his soul be ready, so is his sword." - Mirumoto Yonekura

text: Battle, :gstar:: Equip a Weapon to your target Personality, paying 3 less Gold if he is a Kensai and opposed.
title: A Ready Soul

flavor: "Advance, then recede. Move your army like water, and you will never be left defenseless." - Moto Naleesh

text: Battle: Target your Personality, or one or two of your Personalities if you have fewer Provinces than any other player. Give them each +1F and Conqueror (A Conqueror's unit doesn't bow after battle).
title: Army Like a Tide

flavor: "If my opponent breathes on one, draws on two, and strikes on three, then I aim to strike between two and three. Know your opponent's rhythm and strike when he cannot." - Mirumoto's Niten

text: Battle: You may use an ability on your target Weapon an additional time this turn. Straighten it if it is attached to a Kensai. You may take an additional action.
title: Disrupting the Rhythm

flavor: "You can face me now, or you may face me later. Delay will not make me falter." - Mirumoto Yonekura

text: Iaijutsu Limited: Your target unbowed Personality challenges a target Personality. He may refuse. If he does not refuse, destroy the duel's loser. If he does refuse, permanently give your Personality the ability, "Battle: Challenge a target enemy Personality. Bow the loser."
title: Do Not Delay!

flavor: "It's surprising how many you can fire off at once if you'd rather just scare than kill them." - Tsuruchi Shichiro

text:
Battle: Fear 2 (Bow a target enemy Follower, or Personality without Followers, with 2 or lower Force).
Battle: Bow your target unbowed Scout. Ranged 3 Attack (Destroy a target enemy Follower, or Personality without Followers, with 3 or lower Force).
title: Fearful Volley

flavor: "If there is one brilliant thing that the Dragon have grasped it is this: a soul is never a finished thing. It must always be seeking." - Asako Sadaki

text: Open: Target your unbowed Personality. Look at the top five cards of your Fate deck. You may Equip one of them that is a Weapon to him. Put the remaining cards back in any order. If he is a Kensai and you did not Equip a Weapon, you may put this Strategy back in your hand before the turn ends.
erratum: Errata'd before release to include some missing text.
title: In Stillness, Forge the Soul

flavor: "Know your terrain. Know it so well that it does not matter if you cannot see it." - Daidoji Tametaka

text:
Ranged Attacks from any player's cards have -2 strength unless he controls a Scout at this battlefield.
Battle/Engage: Destroy a Terrain (if able). Put this Terrain into play.
title: Mist

flavor: "I will see this insult answered."

text: Battle: Give your target defending or your target opposed Personality +1F for each card in his unit (including himself). After this Attack Phase ends, if he was defending and you have fewer Provinces than the Attacker, declare an additional Attack Phase against him, during which your other Personalities cannot attack.
title: Reprisal

flavor: A good yojimbo draws the eye.

text: Absent Battle: If he would be opposed, move your target unbowed Personality at home to the current battlefield. If he moved and is a Yojimbo, make a Fear effect with strength equal to the highest Chi among all Courtiers and Shugenja you control (at any location). (You may take Absent actions without presence.)
title: The Greater Threat

flavor: "Take cover, my lady! I will deal with these dogs in a manner they deserve!"

text: Battle: Bow your target unbowed Personality. Fear with strength equal to his Force. If he is a Yojimbo, and you control a Courtier or Shugenja (at any location), after each card bows from the Fear, destroy it.
title: Unassailable Defense

flavor: "Every man is guilty of something. One simply needs to dig deep enough to find it."

text: Battle: Bow your target unbowed Personality. Bow a target enemy card. Its controller discards a card at random if your Personality is a Magistrate.
title: Uncovering the Culprit

flavor: "This tiny box. All that you were, all that you had, you threw away for the contents of this tiny box. Was your life worth so little?"

text: Iaijutsu Battle: Your target unbowed Personality challenges a target enemy Personality. Bow the loser. Destroy him if he is defending.
title: Weakness Exposed

flavor: When fighting against a crowd, two swords are the superior choice.
designernotes: This is a fight between one Kensai and three attackers, and the Kensai takes them all down like an effing champ. The same fight scene should be used for Strike One, Strike Two, and Strike Three.

text: Battle: Target your unbowed Personality. Remove an ability and a boldface keyword (if able) on a card in a target enemy unit. If your Personality is a Kensai, you may bow one of his Weapons to either draw a card, or search your deck for A Kensai's Will, show it, and put it in your hand. You may take an additional action to play A Kensai's Will.
title: A Kensai's Art

flavor: When you decide to attack, keep calm and move quickly, forestalling the enemy attack with a feeling of constant attack from the first moment to the last.
designernotes: See Notes for Strike One. In this art, the enemy the Kensai struck in Strike 1 and Strike 2 should be on the ground and he should be striking the third target.

text: Battle: Bow your target unbowed Personality. Melee Attack equal to his Force minus two. If your Personality was targeted by your A Kensai's Art and A Kensai's Will this turn, you may discard a card to straighten his unit.
title: A Kensai's Victory

flavor: When you take up the sword, you must feel intent upon cutting your enemy.
designernotes: See Notes for Strike One. In this art, the enemy the Kensai attacked in Strike 1 should be laying on the ground and he should be striking a second target.

text: Battle: Target your unbowed Personality. Give a target enemy Follower or Personality -3F. If your Personality was targeted by your A Kensai's Art this turn and he is a Kensai, you may bow his Weapon to either draw a card or search your deck for A Kensai's Victory, show it, and put it in your hand. You may take an additional action to play A Kensai's Victory.
title: A Kensai's Will

flavor: "You have caused great grief with your grasping and scheming, Saigo-san," Karachu said sadly. "It ends now."
use: Kiho, Asako Karachu fighting Yoritomo Saigo
designernotes: [Karachu getting the jump on Saigo from behind.]

text: Air Kiho Battle: Target your unbowed Monk or Shugenja. You may bow him. Move home a target Personality with Force less than or equal to your Monk or Shugenja's Chi, or his Chi plus 3 if you bowed him.
title: Banishing Breath

flavor: "I understand these matters are very difficult, my friends, but I see no reason we cannot come to an agreement that satisfies all parties."
use: High PH, Matsu Yoshito kills Utaku Sung-Ki
designernotes: A Samurai's Honor bring great benefits to his clan.

use: Nitoshi deciding on whether or not to let the creatures of the second pit loose on the Lion
designernotes: Notes: The festering pit of Fu Leng consuming Lion Personalities.

flavor: "Violence is the only language that the creatures of the Shadowlands truly understand. Fortunately, it is one in which the Crab are fluent."
designernotes: Notes: Simple, yet effective.

text: Battle: Target your unbowed Personality. Bow a target enemy card with less Force and without attachments.

designernotes: Art note: A battered, bloodied Crab clan samurai rising up roaring.

text:
As a Focus Effect, if the duel is not during a battle, end the duel without resolution. (Other Focus Effects resolve.)
Battle: Straighten your target opposed card. Give a target Follower or Personality +1F or -1F.

flavor: Dainagon and her apprentice, Doji Natsuyo, made compelling and passionate arguments to move the Colonial Court to Twin Forks City. - Petals and Pincer, Part I
use: Couriter Honor, should have something to do with the Crane attempting to move the Second City court to Twin Forks City
designernotes: Notes - Doji Dainagon and her prodigy [Doji Anti-Scorpion] proposing to move the Colonial Court to Twin Forks City.

text: Political Battle: Bow your target unbowed Courtier. Each other player targets a Personality he controls (if able). Move home all targets. A target player gains or loses 1 Honor.

designernotes: A scout in the bushes scouting her target

text:
Interrupt: Negate an Engage action from a Strategy.
Battle: Give your target opposed Scout +2F.
title: Scout Training

flavor: "I am not surprised the Tsuruchi ambushed you," the commander growled. "I am surprised that you were surprised."

text:
Invest :g1:: Draw a card if the Personality is a Scout. (After this card is played, you may also pay the Invest cost to get the effect, once.)
Absent Interrupt: After your action resolves, if it Recruited your Personality during a battle, take an additional action.

flavor:
I did not even see those last two strikes," Sihung confessed.
Irezu grinned. "But I only struck once, Shinjo-san."
designernotes: 2013 Kotei result. Bayushi Irezu honorably spares Shinjo Sihung

text:
After this Strategy enters your discard pile, give a target Personality -2F.

flavor:
"You are making a mistake!" Jirou hissed.
"I must disagree," Fumiko replied, "the mistake, it would seem, was yours."
designernotes: 2013 Kotei result. Kaiu Fumiko takes Asako Jirou hostage.

text:
Engage: Give a target enemy Personality -2F.

text:

designernotes: Notes: Swans, while known for their beauty, are fierce defenders of their family.

text:
As a Focus Effect, the winner gains 2 Honor.

designernotes: A Crane and Dragon samurai, back to back, practicing their respective dueling styles.

text:
After the first time each turn any of your Personalities wins a duel, give him a +1F token. After your first challenge each turn is refused, gain 1 Honor.

designernotes: 2013 Result. Wrathful Dead kills Kaiu Okura

text: Battle: Give a target enemy Personality -2F. If he has a Poison token, give him a -1F/-1C Poison token.

use: Should get Discipline if you are a Crab Clan Player

text:
If you are a Crab Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g1:.
Battle: Straighten your target Personality. Negate all Force penalties on him. He has +2F while his army is opposed. Destroy him before the turn ends.

flavor: "This is a magnificent blade. Thank you. You have allies among the Crane if you need them." - Doji Makoto
use: Should get Discipline if you are a Crane Clan Player
designernotes: Doji Makoto charismatically charming a group of onlooking dignitaries, while an opponent he has just bested in a duel kneels defeated nearby.

text:
If you are a Crane Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g3:.

use: Should get Discipline if you are a Dragon Clan Player
designernotes: Note: A Mirumoto Kensai staring down a Crane duelist.

text:
If you are a Dragon Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g2:.
Iaijutsu Battle: Your target unbowed Personality challenges a target enemy Personality. Give the winner +2F. Give the loser -2F, and bow him if he is dishonorable.

use: Should get Discipline if you are a Unicorn Clan Player
designernotes: Dairuko looking pristine and honorable

text:
If you are a Lion Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g2:.
Battle: Target your unbowed Personality. Fear equal to his Personal Honor that destroys Followers after it bows them.

designernotes: Note: The Mantis refusing to be defeated.

text:
If you are a Mantis Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g3:.
Battle: Straighten your target Personality. You may use his abilities with Ranged Attack effects an additional time this turn.

flavor: "Some consider me troubled by the circumstances of my childhood. I merely understand that the Phoenix ideals must be maintained regardless of all other considerations. I am, you might say, a purist." - Isawa Koiso
use: Should get Discipline if you are a Phoenix Clan Player
designernotes: A Phoenix Shugenja blasting his enemies with fire.

text:
If you are a Phoenix Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g1:.
Battle: Straighten your target Spell. You may use its abilities an additional time this turn. Take an additional action.

flavor: "The other clans are, at best, convenient allies. They all lack the foresight and conviction to examine the grand scheme of the universe." - Bayushi Nitoshi
use: Should get Discipline if you are a Scorpion Clan Player
designernotes: Bayushi Nitoshi standing contemplatively in a room full of scrolls and papers, with various notations and markings on them. In the foreground, several carved figures representing the other clans have been knocked over or otherwise lay in disarray.

text:
If you are a Scorpion Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g2:.
Open, :g1:: Target a Personality whose Personal Honor is less than printed. His controller loses Honor equal to the difference. Look at two cards in his hand or Provinces.

flavor: "Stand beside me or stand aside. Know that what I set my mind to accomplish, I will achieve. There is no alternative." - Daigotsu Kanpeki
use: Should get Discipline if you are a Spider Clan Player
designernotes: Kanpeki looking incredibly intimidating

text:
If you are a Spider Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g3:.

Interrupt: Give a Fear effect +2 strength. Destroy any Follower after it bows them.
title: Way of the Spider

flavor: "To stand before the Unicorn is to stand in the path of true freedom. We are the children of the wind, and we will not be stifled." - Moto Naleesh
use: Movement based card, should get Discipline if you are a Unicorn Clan Player
designernotes: A detachment of unicorn Cavalry, being led by Moto Naleesh, smashing into the flank of an enemy unit. Her horse is trampling helpless infantry soldiers underfoot. Shinjo Min-hee is at her left side and Utaku Liu-Xeung at her right.

text:
If you are a Unicorn Clan player, this Strategy has Discipline :g3:.
Battle: Target your Personality, who may be at home. Move him home or, if his unit is Cavalry and he would be opposed, move him to the current battlefield.

text: Unstoppable Battle, :g1:: Move home a target defending Personality. (Other players cannot Interrupt Unstoppable actions.)

text:
Courtesy: This Strategy's Ranged Attack has +1 strength. (Courtesy traits do not take effect if you went first.)
Unstoppable Battle, :g1:: Target your unbowed Samurai. Ranged Attack equal to his Personal Honor.

As a Focus Effect, both the winner and loser of this duel gain 1 Honor.
